source_record.title: CD4+ CAR T-cell exhaustion associated with early relapse of multiple myeloma after BCMA CAR T-cell therapy.
source_record.study_context: Observational public-source extraction context after BCMA CAR T-cell therapy in multiple myeloma. This geometry extraction reuses the public source as therapy-pressure navigation context only.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The source reports CAR T-cell persistence and exhaustion-associated immune phenotypes in durable response, transient response, or early relapse contexts after BCMA CAR T-cell therapy, supporting therapy-pressure context as an immune-redirection and therapy-exposure state while preserving product, timepoint, and cohort limits.
limitations: Does not compare products, targets, treatment sequences, or eligibility.
Does not establish patient-specific prognosis, trial fit, or treatment fit.
Uses public-source extraction context and requires full paper-level review before stronger claims.

what_this_supports: The t-cell-therapy-pressure-v0 branch can move to covered-for-v0-navigation as public artifact coverage because it now has one treatment-landscape review extraction and one observational CAR T-cell immune-state extraction.
The geometry should preserve therapy exposure, immune-redirection context, response timing, and immune-cell state as descriptive source fields.
Therapy-pressure context should remain a support layer, not a treatment sequence or product-selection rule.
what_this_does_not_support: It does not recommend CAR T-cell therapy, bispecific antibodies, targets, products, or treatment sequences.
It does not compare BCMA, GPRC5D, or other targets.
It does not infer patient-specific trial fit, therapy fit, prognosis, or relapse risk.
It does not validate clinical actionability or cure claims.

review_notes: Created for Phase 5 discrimination and stress testing.
Source details checked against PubMed metadata for PMID 38574299 and the existing public extraction record.
The extraction intentionally keeps therapy pressure descriptive and non-actionable.
